The pdf_to_num function in pdf-object.c in MuPDF before 1.10 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (use-after-free and application crash) via a crafted file.  NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-7977.  The attack is not for an integer overflow, which was the error in 1.9.5.  The error is in the memleak in the pdf_to_num function.  The memleak occurs because a pointer is not set to NULL when a dictionary entry is removed.  The result is that a non-existent integer dictionary entry can be referenced to determine the type of a subsequent dictionary entry, which allows a type confusion attack.  The type confusion attack leads to a use-after-free via the pdf_to_name function in the same file.  The pdf_t

This could lead to remote information disclosure over Bluetooth with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. rootdir/init.rc in Android 4.x before 4.4.4 does not ensure that the /data/tombstones directory exists for the Debuggerd component, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.

Use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel before 3.3.6, when huge pages are enabled, allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) or possibly gain privileges by interacting with a hugetlbfs filesystem, as demonstrated by a umount operation that triggers improper handling of quota data.  This is related to the page_header and fs_struct updates in fs/hugetlb.c.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-2439.  The recommended workaround is to disable the use of huge pages.  If that is not possible, a backported kernel update is available.  This is rfc 4944, committed 2013-05-14 23:16:25.

The NuPlayer::GenericSource::notifyPreparedAndCleanup function in media/libmediaplayerservice/nuplayer/GenericSource.cpp in mediaserver in Android 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49G and 6.x before 2016-02-01 improperly manages mDrmManagerClient objects, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 25070434. NOTE: this is a Android Media Framework bug, which is distinct from the similar bug in the Android Media Player. We are currently unable to address the Android Media Framework issue for Android 6.x before 2016-03-01 because of incompatible library dependencies. We are addressing this issue for Android 5.1

NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-5143.  The attack is not for an elevation of privilege, but rather a denial of service.

The vmnc decoder in libav allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or execute arbitrary code via a crafted .vmf file that has a large number of delay frames followed by a large number of shapes with the same hotspot and image data.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-7031.  NOTE: this issue is related to an unspecified "memory corruption" issue with a vmnc decoder that allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service by leveraging a crafted .vmf file that has a large number of delay frames.  NOTE: this issue is related to an unspecified "memory corruption" issue with a vmnc decoder that allows local users to cause a denial of service by leveraging a crafted .vmf file that has a large number of delay frames. libxml2 before 2.9.1, when --with-python is enabled, allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via an XML external entity (XXE) attack on an XML file that is read by an invalid UTF-8 string in a text node. The problem is that the XML parser does not properly consider the UTF-8 validation as a sanity check for a larger memory allocation, which allows attacker to trigger a buffer overflow. This is different from CVE-2012-1847. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-1847. The suggested work-around for CVE-2012-1847 is not effective in all cases for this vulnerability. The recommended way to protect against this vulnerability is to avoid using invalid UTF-8 strings in text nodes in XML files. Confirming with the application vendor that the XM

This issue is related to an issue that was found in Android 5.0 before 2016-03-01 in which a crafted device can bypass a pairing attempt due to a missing pairing confirmation. The js_parse_function_str function in jsparse.c in Artifex Software, Inc. MuJS before 1.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read and application crash) via a crafted string. The vulnerability exists because a zero-byte is not properly enforced during certain error handling. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2013-7345.

libvpx in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, related to libwebm/mkvparser.cpp and other files, aka internal bug 23452792. This vulnerability exists because a lack of size check. Finish function processes a slice size that is too large and memory accesses may cross the bound of the accesses by a few bytes, leading to a read or write outside the block or an out-of-bound array access. (The affected apps all use the Netflix Android app for common capabilities.) NOTE: this issue became relevant after the Android 4.

The exim_store_auth_user function in auth/auth_exim.c in Exim before 4.82.1, when used with dovecot for IMAP authentication, allows remote attackers to determine user existence via a (1) MAIL FROM or (2) RCPT TO command that contains a double quote character, as demonstrated by a (3) MAIL FROM "foo"<bar> command that reveals a syntax error.

The Enhanced Metafile (EMF) parser in the GDI in Microsoft Windows 10 and Server 2012 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (Out-of-bounds Read) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted document, aka "Windows Graphical Device Interface Library Remote Denial of Service Vulnerability." This is related to the Windows GDI library's emf.c file and the ExtTextOut function. This vulnerability is rated as Moderate because it requires an uncommon device configuration. Product: Windows. Versions: 10.0.0.14393.0, 10.0.10240.0, 10.0.14393.0, 10.0.14723.0, 10.0.15063.

The prctl_set_vma_anon_name function in kernel/sys.c in Android before 5.1.1 LMY49F and 6.0 before 2016-01-01 does not ensure that only one vma is accessed in a certain update action, which allows attackers to gain privileges or cause a denial of service (vma list corruption) via a crafted application, aka internal bug 20017123.  NOTE: this is a unique case of a race condition where the application may need to be started again after a kernel update.  The other typical need for restart is handled using a system reboot.  Android ID 44746244.  NOTE: this issue is rated as Moderate because it could be used by an attacker to gain local access to a privileged process.  Android ID 44

A malicious webview could install long-lived unload handlers that re-use an incognito BrowserContext that is queued for destruction in versions of Oxide before 1.18.3. This can result in a use-after-free and potentially code execution. This flaw is related to a use-after-free in the Bluetooth protocol handler in the Networking Engine. NOTE: this is a generic class of issue that can be fixed by aoxide developers and is not a vulnerability in the Android operating system. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by sending a crafted request to aoxide component. This vulnerability is rated as High because it could be used to execute arbitrary code in the context of a privileged process. Product: Android. Versions: 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-389391

NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-8895. The second fix for CVE-2017-8896 makes it easier for remote attackers to cause a denial of service (integer overflow and application crash) via a crafted MP3 file. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). DTD handlers (such as the one for the "DOCTYPE" declaration) are responsible for this issue. This occurs in tree.c:2624. Not exposed if the document is loaded from an URL and the "DOCTYPE" declaration is in an external subset. Not exposed in Java when using the XMLParser or SAX2XMLReader. Java, code not yet released. Not exposed in PHP before 5.5.38, 5.6.21, and 7

An elevation of privilege vulnerability in the Android media framework (audio hal). Product: Android. Versions: 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-62873231. The audio HAL module has signature capabilities but is not signatured. This could enable a local module to run as the HAL in the absence of a proper HAL. This could allow the local module to read system files, write in /data, or even execute code inside the HAL. This is a privilege escalation vulnerability, which can be triggered by a factory reset. libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require use of the ICameraService::dump method for a camera service dump, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly dumps, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26265403.

fs/cifs/cifssmb.c in the Linux kernel before 3.1 does not properly handle context-switching, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted application.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2008-5531.  The suggested workaround does not address the underlying issue. An elevation of privilege vulnerability in the Android media framework (audio hal). Product: Android. Versions: 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-62873231. The issue is that due to a missing domain attribute check, a privileged process can gain privileges by binding to an unprivileged process of the same uid. This occurs because the unprivileged process default-handles a AUDIO_IO_DEVICE_ADDED action, which the privileged process is not expected to handle. This is different state than the privileged process and is a valid device, which can be abused. A privileged process can then call dumpsys to obtain sensitive information. This is a device-specific issue that was only visible in firmware where audio hal is different. User interaction is not needed for exploitation

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). Default parser flags can be set when creating a XML parser context, e.g., with xmlCreatePushParserContext(). This is a recurring issue in XML parsers that use default parser flags if no explicit flags are set. Applications and libraries are recommended to use xmlCreatePushParserContext() with a non-zero value for the _vc_flags_ parameter to prevent the use of external entitities with the XML parser. Libxml2 before 2.9.1 and 2

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). Default parser flags are used in scenarios where the caller does not have a say in parser flags, provided the caller did not request a specific subset of features. For example, default parser flags are used in XInclude (when the XInclude processor implements namespace binding with XML namespaces). When an application does not use XInclude, then the application is not vulnerable. In de_dotdot of de.c, there is a pathname traversal vulnerability due to access beyond the end of a source string. It allows an attacker to access arbitrary files on the system by leveraging the world-readable files in /etc. This issue is similar to CVE-2016-7208. The vulnerability exists in Artifex Ghostscript before 9.21. The issue is triggered by a crafted Postscript document. It is not related to the use of the Ghostscript library in an application. The attack vector is to send a crafted document to the Ghostscript executable. It is not possible to exploit this vulnerability over a network. This issue is only possible when an attacker has permission to write files on the system. The recommended solution is to upgrade to Ghostscript 9.21. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). Default parser flags are used in scenarios where the caller does not have a say in parser flags, such as in XML parsing from a socket. Doing so can potentially expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags. It is not known whether this issue exists in third-party libxml2 bindings for different languages. Android is not affected as it uses a different XML parsing library. The encode_share_access function in fs/nfs/nfs4xdr.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.29 allows local users to cause a denial of service (BUG and system crash) by using the mknod system call with a pathname on an NFSv4 filesystem.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2010-3865.  NOTE: this may be the same as CVE-2010-3870.  NOTE: this has been SPLIT into several CVEs based on the affected code paths.  The details are that encode_change_attr4_to_update and encode_open_to_downgrade4_to_read_only do not check for the deny_only flag, which allows a share_access violation.

NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-4092.  The fix in 9.0-RELEASE-p3, when applied to a 9.0-RELEASE-p1 system, is not a bump in version number due to being a security update.

The main function in this C program creates a temporary file using the xfmkstemp function, but does not remove it before exiting. This can cause the file to be orphaned and potentially be removed or overwritten by the system. This is a vulnerability because it could be used to expose a file intended for use by another process, or to expose data in a temporary file to an attacker who could use it to bypass a sandbox or other security mechanism. The recommended action is to remove the orphaned file before the program exits. This vulnerability could be used by an attacker to bypass a sandbox or other security mechanism. Session fixation vulnerability in the Sessions subsystem in PHP before 5.5.2 allows remote attackers to hijack web sessions by specifying a session ID. The vulnerability exists because a random session ID generation is incomplete, which allows an attacker to guess the session ID to hijack. The issue is related to 5.4.x before 5.4.31, 5.5.x before 5.5.16, and 5.6.x before 5.6.4. It does not occur in 5.4.32, 5.5.20, or 5.6.6 because the session ID cannot be guessed from the request headers. The session ID can be guessed from the request headers with certain HTTP clients and servers that do not implement RFC 2109 correctly. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). Libxml2 is used in many applications, including Apache XML-Catalog, Catalog Manager, and BSD pkg. This flaw is different from CVE-2016-5141. It also exists in libxml2 before 2.9.3. A malicious webview could install long-lived unload handlers that re-use an incognito BrowserContext that is queued for destruction in versions of Oxide before 1.18.3. This will cause the BrowserContext to be re-used, and any pages loaded in it will have access to the previously loaded pages in the context. This can expose information and more importantly it can be used to bypass CSP restrictions and read sensitive data. It can also be used to create a channel to a malicious JavaScript context. To mitigate this the application should destroy the BrowserContext as soon as it is not needed anymore. We are unaware of any real-world exploitation of this issue. This is rated as High due to the use of an incognito BrowserContext which is unique to a specific application and not typically exposed to the user. Product: Android. Versions: 1.17.1, 1.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). The IKEv2 parser in tcpdump before 4.9.2 has a buffer over-read in print-isakmp.c, several functions. The impact is unknown, but probably not good. The value is used to compute an length value that is used as an array index. (A similar issue exists in print-ipsec.c.) The environment in which tcpdump is used may cause this to be exploitable. A remote attacker may be able to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted packet. This vulnerability exists because the IKEv2 parser does not check the packet length before reading beyond the end of the packet. This is related to an issue fixed in OpenBSD 6

ImageMagick before 7.0.8-50 has a *use of uninitialized value* vulnerability in the function ReadCUTImage in coders/cut.c. This occurs when a CUT file has too few pixels. The value of pixels is used as an array index. If a zero value is found, it is read as an unsigned char, which is then multiplied with 4. If the zero value is the first in the array, the product is zero, and the check in ReadCUTImage that the product is less than the allocated size will pass. If the zero value is not the first in the array, the product will be 4, and the check will fail, and the function will return a NULL image. This could lead to a crash of the application that uses ImageMagick. It could also lead to a denial of service by making the application think the image is of a different size than it is. Sepa

In AMediaCodecCryptoInfo_new of NdkMediaCodec.cpp, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution in external apps with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111603051 Internal bug: tba External bug: 449873795428736-111603051/111603051 (111603051 and 449873795428736) Android ID: A-1116030

Das U-Boot versions 2016.09 through 2019.07-rc4 can memset() too much data while reading a crafted ext4 filesystem, which results in a stack buffer overflow and likely code execution. This is because the default allocation for the lb_len field in the btree block in ext4 is 12 bits, but only 11 bits are actually used, which allows an attacker to craft a malicious ext4 filesystem that, when mounted by the Linux kernel, causes a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly even execution of code. The issue is in fs/ext4/extents.c, and is related to ext4_get_memcpy_len() and ext4_get_blk_rec_pref(). The flaw is in the calculation of the memcpy size, and not in the way that the function uses memcpy. To make

In ImageMagick before 7.0.8-25, a memory leak exists in WritePSDChannel in coders/psd.c. It is triggered by a large amount of data, and leads to a use-after-free. It was not always exploitable, because of an unrelated bug that was fixed in the same commit (Integer overflow in the PhaseRound function in coders/png.c). This could be exploited to bypass a sandbox and write to a file. It is fixed in 7.0.8-25. The recommended solution is to update to that version of ImageMagick. It is also possible to build ImageMagick from source with the --enable-openmp option disabled, which will disable OpenMP support and remove the bug. This will not be necessary in ImageMagick 7.0.9, which will have a different, improved PhaseRound implementation. The bug wa

An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 4.20.15. The nfc_llcp_build_tlv function in net/nfc/llcp_commands.c may return NULL. If the caller does not check for this, it will trigger a NULL pointer dereference. This will cause denial of service. This affects nfc_llcp_build_gb in net/nfc/llcp_core.c.

An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 4.20.15. The nfc_llcp_build_tlv function in net/nfc/llcp_commands.c may return NULL. If the caller does not check for this, it will trigger a NULL pointer dereference. This will cause denial of service. This affects nfc_llcp_build_gb in net/nfc/llcp_core.c.

The hw_breakpoint_slot_get function in arch/x86/kernel/hw_breakpoint.c in the Linux kernel before 3.1 does not properly verify the ID of a breakpoint slot, which allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka Android internal bug 28146147.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-0849.

An issue was discovered in rds_tcp_kill_sock in net/rds/tcp.c in the Linux kernel before 5.0.8. There is a race condition leading to a use-after-free, related to net namespace cleanup. This leads to a denial of service (system crash) or possibly other unspecified impact. This occurs because sk_sleep does not require that the socket be locked, leading to a situation where a free can run concurrently with another writer. This is related to an invalid read in a different path, which triggeres an extra copy. Differently from the previous race, here the extra copy makes it easier to trigger the bug with a simple application. To be mitigated.

The tt_face_load_kern function in sfnt/ttkern.c in FreeType before 2.5.4 enforces an incorrect minimum table length, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted TrueType font.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-2240.  NOTE: this is related to an issue that was supposed to be fixed in 2.5.2.

The IVD_DECODE_PROC function in media/libmediaplayerservice/IVDCryptoAPI.cpp in mediaserver in Android 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not initialize a certain key-request data structure, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, via unspecified vectors, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26323455. This is related to the android.media.CryptoInfo.numKeyRequests field and the ivdcrypto::IIVDCrypto::processKeyRequests function. NOTE: this affects Android 6.x before 2016-03-01 as well. This is related to the __mincore function and the fs/proc/task_mmu.c file.

The ARP parser in tcpdump before 4.9.2 has a buffer over-read in print-arp.c, several functions. The impact is unknown, as tcpdump is not typically run on end-user systems. However, the remote attack vector is an attack on another system upon which tcpdump is running, and the installed version of libpcap is before 1.0.0. This can be addressed with a patch. The recommended approach is to either install libpcap 1.0.0 or higher, or update tcpdump to a version that is not vulnerable to this issue. Note that libpcap 1.0.0 was released over two years ago; the recommended approach is to use a version that is not vulnerable to this issue. The Windows kernel, as used in Windows Server 2003 and Windows XP, does not properly perform event handling, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted application that triggers an excessive number of events.  This is related to the hFaultLock in kernel/fault.c.  NOTE: this issue became relevant after the CVE-2009-1132 patch.  The current hotfix (as of 2009-10-14) is also relevant.  For more information, see https://support.microsoft.com/kb/266fa6d9-066b-4f1f-84f2-4f32bd82e832.

The event scripts in Automatic Bug Reporting Tool (ABRT) uses world-readable permission on a copy of sosreport file in problem directories, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from /var/log/messages via unspecified vectors. This issue is related to abr-handler-event.py and abr-sosreport.py. It exists because abr-sosreport.py uses a copy2 function with a world-readable permission. It is not a feature, but a bug. The bug is present in Python 2.6.6, 2.7.8, and 3.4.3. It is also present in abrt-2.1.10, abrt-addon-ccpp-2.0.6, and abrt-addon-kernel-oops-2.1.1. It is fixed in Python 2.7.10

The encode_share_access function in fs/nfs/nfs4xdr.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.29 allows local users to cause a denial of service (BUG and system crash) by using the mknod system call with a pathname on an NFSv4 filesystem.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2010-5321.

In ImageMagick before 7.0.8-25, a memory leak exists in WritePSDChannel in coders/psd.c. It is triggered by a large amount of data and results in a significant memory usage increase. It was not properly disposed of after a fork. This could be used to cause a denial of service by making many small files. A remote attacker could use this flaw to cause a denial of service over a network. This is related to forking and memory usage. It can be triggered by a large amount of data. It is unrelated to the Heap-based Buffer Overflow that was discovered in ImageMagick. That vulnerability was in the function ReadImage in coders/svg.c. It was fixed in ImageMagick 7.0.8-25. It was not properly deallocate memory in the 'end' function. An attack vector is a forked parent process. I

The YAML parser in YAML 1.1 before 1.1.50 does not properly process %YAML tags in conjunction with %TAG hashes, which might allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted YAML document, a similar issue to CVE-2012-2241.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-2240.  NOTE: this is a vulnerability in the YAML implementation in Python, not in the underlying libyaml library.  The libyaml library is used by many applications, and this vulnerability could be used to cause denial-of-service conditions or possibly code execution for an attacker who is able to load YAML data. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This is a compilation of details from several reports, with some additional details and context. The bug is a race condition in the WindowManager code that allows a malicious application to access private data in a specific situation where a malicious application and a trusted application both register a window for drawing, and the trusted application returns false from a Choreographer.Callback implementation, which causes the WindowManager to apply the window's surface before the Choreographer has finished processing events for the current frame. An attacker-controlled application can then access the arbitrary private-storage file by tapping a button in the permission dialog. Android ID: A-3

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. This is because the *swell_is_string_all_digits()* function in swell.c does not properly limit the amount of data it reads while searching for a 'delimiter' character, and this can be used to cause a buffer overflow when the 'delimiter' is found inside a string. Applications that use libsndfile before 1.0.28 should be updated to version 1.0.28 or later in order to benefit from this security fix. Use-after-free vulnerability in Google Chrome before 14.0.835.202 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact via vectors related to the Google V8 bindings. In Android, a bind to the v8::Context::Global proxy is not properly handled. This is related to v8/src/api.cc, v8/src/bootstrapper.cc, and v8/src/context.cc. Google has not announced any patch version for this issue. Note: third-party browsers are not affected. Android ID: A-32377371.

Google V8, as used in Google Chrome before 14.0.835.163, does not properly perform object sealing, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact via vectors that leverage *type confusion.* *Type confusion* can result in *data corruption* because an attacker-controlled value can be modified by a *plain object* assignment. NOTE: this vulnerability only exists because of a lack of proper sealing in Google Chrome. Other vendors also use a *plain object* for *object sealing*, which may be *same-origin checked* to prevent unauthorized property addition.

Google V8, as used in Google Chrome before 14.0.835.163, does not properly perform object sealing, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact via vectors that leverage *type confusion.* *Type confusion* occurs because non-extensible objects are indistinguishable from extensible objects with different names and call signatures. *Type confusion* can also occur in other areas, leading to *data corruption* or *memory corruption.* *Data corruption* or *memory corruption* can result in code execution. NOTE: this issue became relevant after the Mozilla Firefox team discovered that Google V8 is used in the same situation.

Use-after-free vulnerability in Google Chrome before 14.0.835.202 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact via vectors related to the Google V8 bindings. Google V8, as used in Google Chrome before 14.0.835.163, does not properly perform object sealing, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service or possibly have unspecified other impact via vectors that leverage *type confusion.* *Type confusion* can result in *data corruption* because an attacker-controlled value can be modified as if it were a different data type. *Type confusion* can happen during *property deletion* because the *IsSmi* concept is not respected throughout the JavaScript engine. NOTE: this issue exists because Google V8's implementation of ECMAScript is incomplete. It is available in Google Chrome before 14.0.835.163. *This issue has been classified as High severity due to the possibility of remote code execution and the possibility of data corruption.*

Google Chrome before 14.0.835.202 does not properly handle Google V8 hidden objects, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted JavaScript code. This is related to but different from CVE-2016-5773. NOTE: this issue became relevant after the vulnerability in CRLF handling was identified in OpenSSL. In OpenSSL 1.0.2h, CRLF processing of certificates is fixed, but it is less easy to exploit because the crafted context is no longer the same one in which CRLFs are actually significant. In OpenSSL 1.0.2g, CRLF processing of certificates is not fixed. Workarounds are in OpenSSL 1.0.2f. Also, OpenSSL 1.1.0 is not affected

Google Chrome before 14.0.835.163 does not properly handle Khmer characters, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via unspecified vectors. This issue is rated as Moderate because it requires an invalid DUKPT configuration in order to cause glibc to return an error, which potentially allows attackers to cause a denial of service (use-after-free) via crafted USB traffic. Google Chrome before 14.0.835.202 does not properly handle Google V8 hidden objects, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted JavaScript code. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-3594. NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2014-4630 because of a different vulnerability type.

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. This is because the *swallow_metadata()* function (common.c) does not properly limit the number of iterations while reading keyword-value metadata. A user on Reddit discovered this vulnerability after shipping a "patched" version of libsndfile to debian. The debian package was not signed, so a malicious DEB file could have been put online and users could have unintentionally installed a malicious version of the library. This update fixes this issue by adding a limit to the number of iterations.
